log_table.send_requests_hint: Logs will appear here after you send some requests

log_detail.title: Request Details
log_detail.stream_merge_unavailable: Unable to derive a merged view right now. Switch to Raw to inspect the original content.

upstream_manager.title: Manage Upstreams
upstream_manager.name: Name (Subdomain)
upstream_manager.confirm_delete: Are you sure you want to delete the forwarding config for subdomain "{{name}}"?
upstream_manager.no_upstreams: No upstreams configured. Add one above.

settings.page_description: Manage proxy entrypoints, upstream routes, logging capture, and storage policy in one place. The page is regrouped by workflow so it no longer reads like a giant flattened form.
settings.access_description: PrismCat forwards requests by subdomain by default, and can optionally expose a path-based route on the current dashboard host. Configure both entry styles here.
settings.upstreams_description: Treat upstreams as a list instead of a stack of mini cards. Each route keeps only the core information: name, target, entry URL, and timeout.
settings.logging_description: Control body truncation, detachment, previews, and header masking so logs stay useful without letting storage costs sprawl.
settings.storage_description: Adjust retention and review where the current database is stored on disk.
settings.copy_proxy_url: Click to copy proxy endpoint URL
settings.copy_path_proxy_url: Click to copy path routing endpoint URL
settings.proxy_domain_suffix_hint: After you add an upstream, requests can be forwarded through entrypoints like {{name}}.{{suffix}}.
settings.enable_path_routing_hint: When enabled, PrismCat also accepts requests through a reserved path prefix on the current dashboard host, for example /_proxy/openai/v1.
settings.path_routing_prefix_hint: Use a dedicated prefix to avoid collisions with UI/API routes. Empty values fall back to /_proxy.
settings.path_entry_hint: When enabled, this route is also available through the reserved prefix on the current dashboard host.
settings.path_entry_disabled: Path routing is currently disabled. Save this section to enable the route.
settings.max_request_body_hint: Request bodies larger than this will be truncated
settings.max_response_body_hint: Response bodies larger than this will be truncated
settings.sensitive_headers_hint: These header values will be masked in logs
settings.detach_body_over_bytes_hint: Bodies larger than this will be stored as blobs on disk instead of DB (set to 0 to disable)
settings.body_preview_bytes_hint: Number of KB to keep inline in DB as a preview when a body is detached
settings.database_path_hint: This setting requires editing the config file and restarting
settings.early_request_body_snapshot_hint: When enabled, PrismCat saves an extra snapshot as soon as the request body is fully sent to the upstream (even if the upstream hasn't responded yet). This makes request bodies visible earlier, but adds an extra DB write per request. Disable to improve throughput (request bodies will only appear after a request completes).
settings.store_base64_hint: When enabled, Base64 encoded data (e.g. images) in bodies will be stored. Disable to significantly reduce storage overhead.

playground.title: Playground
playground.description: Send requests to configured upstreams, or replay requests from logs
playground.empty_state: Configure your request and send it
playground.empty_state_hint: Select an upstream, fill in the path and body, then click Send or press Ctrl+Enter
